Traditionally the fourth of the four seasons, typically regarded as spanning the months of December, January, and February in temperate and polar regions of the Northern Hemisphere or the months of June, July, and August in the Southern Hemisphere. It is the time when the sun is lowest in the sky, resulting in short days, and the time of year with the lowest atmospheric temperatures for the region.

examples.english: Winter is my favorite season.

A piece of such material; a thread or slender rod of metal, a cable.

(ergative) To turn over in one's mind, as of deep thoughts; to (cause to) be considered thoroughly.
(programming) To perform an operation similar to a bit shift, but with the bit that "falls off the end" being wrapped around to the other end.
(intransitive) To make a loud or heavy rumbling noise.
A fulfilment of a need or desire.
The pleasure obtained by such fulfillment.
The source of such gratification.
A reparation for an injury or loss.
A vindication for a wrong suffered.
A particular discipline or branch of knowledge that is natural, measurable or consisting of systematic principles rather than intuition or technical skill. [from 14th c.]

examples.english: In recent years, science has made remarkable progress.

current_entries.en_definition: The collective discipline of study or learning acquired through the scientific method; the sum of knowledge gained from such methods and discipline. [from 18th c.]
(uncountable, archaic) Knowledge gained through study or practice; mastery of a particular discipline or area. [from 14th c.]
(now, only, _, theology) The fact of knowing something; knowledge or understanding of a truth. [from 14th c.]
The condition of not being threatened, especially physically, psychologically, emotionally, or financially.

examples.english: Please get me hotel security.

current_entries.en_definition: Something that secures.
(legal) Something that secures the fulfillment of an obligation or law.
(finance, often, in plural) A tradeable financial asset, such as a share of stock or any of various classes of bond.W
(finance) Property etc. temporarily relinquished to guarantee repayment of a loan.
Being the only one of the kind; unique.
Distinguished by superiority: peerless, unmatched, eminent, exceptional, extraordinary.
(linear algebra, of transformation) Having the property that the matrix of coefficients of the new variables has a determinant equal to zero.
(grammar) Referring to only one thing or person.
(linear algebra, of matrix) Having no inverse.
(grammar) A form of a word that refers to only one person or thing.
The state of reduced consciousness during which a human or animal rests in a daily rhythm.

examples.english: I have to go to sleep.

current_entries.en_definition: (countable, informal) An act or instance of sleeping.
Rheum, crusty or gummy discharge found in the corner of the eyes after waking, whether real or a figurative objectification of sleep (in the sense of reduced consciousness).
(intransitive) To rest in a state of reduced consciousness.
(intransitive) To be, or appear to be, in repose; to be quiet; to be unemployed, unused, or unagitated; to rest; to lie dormant.
(of a person) Regretful or apologetic for one's actions.

examples.english: I'm sorry, I can't stay long.

current_entries.en_definition: (of a person) Grieved or saddened, especially by the loss of something or someone.
Poor, pitifully sad or regrettable.
Expresses regret, remorse, or sorrow.
Used as a request for someone to repeat something not heard or understood clearly.
Used to correct oneself in speech.
Exercising power of rule.

examples.english: Puerto Rico is not a sovereign state.

current_entries.en_definition: A monarch; the ruler of a country.
A gold coin of the United Kingdom, with a nominal value of one pound sterling but in practice used as a bullion coin.
One who is not a subject to a ruler or nation.
A very large champagne bottle with the capacity of about 25 liters, equivalent to 33+1‚ĀĄ3 standard bottles.
(biology, taxonomy) A category in the classification of organisms, ranking below genus; a taxon at that rank.
A group of plants or animals in which any two individuals of the appropriate sexes or mating types can produce fertile offspring, typically by sexual reproduction, usually having similar appearance.
(mineralogy) A mineral with a unique chemical formula whose crystals belong to a unique crystallographic system.
(Christianity) Either of the two elements of the Eucharist after they have been consecrated.
Having a substance; actually existing.
Large in size, quantity, or value; ample; significant.
Most important; essential.
Satisfying; having sufficient substance to be nourishing or filling.
Not imaginary; real; actual; true; veritable.
Corporeal; material; firm.
(sometimes, attributive) Something which supports.

examples.english: You are now old enough to support yourself.

current_entries.en_definition: Financial or other help.
Answers to questions and resolution of problems regarding something sold.
(transitive) To help keep from falling.
(transitive) To help, particularly financially; to subsidize.
(transitive, said of electronic devices, programming languages, etc.) To be designed to provide capacity for; to work or be compatible with (a part, accessory, file type, program, algorithm, etc.).
(intransitive) Of a person, to continue to live; to remain alive.
(transitive) To live longer than (someone); to outlive (someone or something); to outlast (something).
(intransitive) Of an object or concept, to continue to exist.
(transitive) To live past (a life-threatening event)
(Greek god) A god of the sea, son of Poseidon.

(particle) the nucleus of a tritium atom, consisting of a proton and two neutrons.
Any of several marine gastropods of the family Ranellidae, which have a pointed spiral shell.
Able to vary or be varied.

current_entries.en_definition: Marked by diversity or difference.
(mathematics) Having no fixed quantitative value.
Something that is variable.
Something whose value may be dictated or discovered.
(mathematics) A quantity that may assume any one of a set of values.
(mathematics) A symbol representing a variable.
(programming) A named memory location in which a program can store intermediate results and from which it can read them.
(historical) A small horse-drawn carriage for two people sitting facing each other.
A person holding a corresponding position in another organisation.
In relation to; compared with.
Opposite; across from; set so as to be facing.
In what situation.

current_entries.en_definition: In, at or to the place (that) or a place (that).
In a/the situation, position, case, etc. in which.

The place in which something happens.
Not having any wires.

A village in Tavrychanka rural hromada, Kakhovka Raion, Kherson Oblast, Ukraine, founded in 1902.

A village in Holovne settlement hromada, Kovel Raion, Volyn Oblast, Ukraine, already well established by 1906.
(music) The minor key with A as its tonic, consisting of the pitches A, B, C, D, E, F, and G.
(music) The minor chord with a root of A.
(transitive) To lower, as in condition in life, office, rank, etc., so as to cause pain or hurt feelings; to degrade, to depress, to humble, to humiliate. [from c. 1350‚Äď1470]

A monastery or society of people, secluded from the world and devoted to religion and celibacy, which is headed by an abbot or abbess; also, the monastic building or buildings. [First attested around 1150 to 1350.]
The church of a monastery. [First attested around 1150 to 1350.]
Shortened; made briefer.
Relatively short; shorter than normal, or compared to others.
An abridgment; a compendium; an abstract. [since the late 16th century]
A shortened form of a word or phrase, used in place of the whole; an abbreviation. [since the mid 17th century]

The fundamentals of any subject. [First attested in the mid-16th century.]

(transitive, obsolete) To reject; to cast off; to discard. [mid 16th ‚Äď late 17th c.]

(transitive) To surrender, renounce or relinquish, as sovereign power; to withdraw definitely from filling or exercising, as a high office, station, dignity; to fail to fulfill responsibility for. [from mid 17th c.]
(intransitive) To relinquish or renounce a throne, or other high office or dignity; to renounce sovereignty. [First attested in the early 18th c.]
Existing in or sunk to a low condition, position, or state; contemptible, despicable, miserable. [from early 15th c.]
Of a person: cast down in hope or spirit; showing utter helplessness, hopelessness, or resignation; also, grovelling; ingratiating; servile. [from mid 14th c.]
(historical) To renounce (something) upon oath; to forswear; specifically, to recant or retract (a heresy or some other opinion); to withdraw. [from 15th c.]
To solemnly reject (someone or something); to abandon (someone or something) forever; to disavow, to disclaim, to repudiate. [from 15th c.]
(obsolete) A carrying or taking away; removal. [First attested around 1350 to 1470.]
(surgery) The surgical removal of a body part, an organ, or especially a tumor; the removal of an organ function; amputation. [First attested around 1350 to 1470.]
